vBRIEFInfo.description: Adoption blocker: run.bat resolves the `run` Python entry point against the caller's CWD instead of %~dp0, breaking the documented `..\deft\run upgrade` flow on Windows.

plan.title: run.bat: resolve `run` against %~dp0 instead of CWD (Windows)

narratives.Problem: run.bat:25 reads `python.exe run %*`. The `run` token is an unqualified relative path; Windows resolves it against the current working directory of the caller, not the directory of run.bat. Consumers who install deft as `./deft/` and follow the documented `..\deft\run upgrade` invocation from their project root therefore see python.exe fail to find a script named `run`. Workaround in field use: `python.exe "<full path>\deft\run" upgrade`.
narratives.Overview: Single-line fix in run.bat: replace `python.exe run %*` with `python.exe "%~dp0run" %*`. %~dp0 expands to the drive+path of the executing batch file with a trailing backslash and is the canonical Windows idiom for self-relative path resolution.
narratives.Background: The Python entry point (`run`) lives next to run.bat in the deft repo root. The launcher worked when invoked from inside the deft repo (CWD == deft repo root) but never did when invoked as `..\deft\run.bat <cmd>` from a sibling consumer project, which is the documented install pattern.
narratives.Constraint: MUST preserve compatibility with existing in-deft-repo invocations (running run.bat help from the deft repo root must continue to work). MUST NOT regress the Python version detection / Microsoft Store install fallback paths in run.bat.
narratives.Risk: Very low. The change is one line, the idiom is well-known, and the existing in-repo behavior is preserved as a special case of the general rule.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) `..\deft\run.bat upgrade` succeeds from a sibling directory; (2) C:\path\to\deft\run.bat <cmd> succeeds from any CWD; (3) existing in-repo invocation continues to work; (4) regression test or smoke check covers the cross-CWD case.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_run_bat_path.py (new, Windows-conditional via @pytest.mark.skipif on platform): cd to a temp directory not equal to the deft repo root, invoke run.bat help via subprocess.run, assert returncode == 0 and stdout contains 'Usage:' or 'Deft CLI'. On non-Windows, the test skips.
narratives.Action: 1. Edit run.bat line 25 to use %~dp0run. 2. Add tests/cli/test_run_bat_path.py with the cross-CWD smoke test (skipped off Windows). 3. Run task check to confirm no regression. 4. CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Fixed.
narratives.Urgency: Adoption blocker. Every Windows consumer following the documented upgrade flow hits this on first contact. Blast radius is small (one file edit) and fix is mechanical.

narrative.Action: Replace `python.exe run %*` with `python.exe "%~dp0run" %*`. Preserve the surrounding Python version detection (lines 4-22) and Microsoft Store fallback (lines 28-40) verbatim.

Skipped off Windows. Use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory, subprocess.run with cwd=tempdir invoking the absolute path to run.bat with arg 'help', assert returncode == 0. Pin to pytest fixtures already used in tests/cli/.
One-line entry: fix(run): run.bat now resolves the run entry point against %~dp0 so `..\\deft\\run.bat <cmd>` works from any CWD on Windows (#791).

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate per main.md Decision Making bullet. The new test file MUST be present before commit (forward coverage rule).
